Version
  • Bibliography
  • Bibliography

Bibliography

References used in the development, validation, and parameterisation of GreenhousesJL.

Greenhouse climate modelling

  • Altes-Buch, Q., Quoilin, S. & Lemort, V. (2019). Greenhouses: A Modelica Library for the Simulation of Greenhouse Climate and Energy Systems. Proceedings of the 13th International Modelica Conference, Regensburg. doi:10.3384/ecp19157533

  • Vanthoor, B.H.E. et al. (2011a). A methodology for model-based greenhouse design: Part 1, a greenhouse climate model for a broad range of designs and climates. Biosystems Engineering, 110(4), 363–377. doi:10.1016/j.biosystemseng.2011.08.012

  • Vanthoor, B.H.E. et al. (2011b). A methodology for model-based greenhouse design: Part 2, description and validation of a tomato yield model. Biosystems Engineering, 110(4), 378–395. doi:10.1016/j.biosystemseng.2011.08.013

  • Katzin, D. et al. (2020). GreenLight, An open source model for greenhouses with supplemental lighting. Biosystems Engineering, 194, 61–81. doi:10.1016/j.biosystemseng.2020.03.010

  • de Zwart, H.F. (1996). Analysing energy-saving options in greenhouse cultivation using a simulation model. PhD thesis, Wageningen University.

Heat transfer & convection

  • Bot, G.P.A. (1983). Greenhouse climate: from physical processes to a dynamic model. PhD thesis, Wageningen University. WUR Library

  • Balemans, L. (1989). Assessment of criteria for energetic effectiveness of greenhouse screens. PhD thesis, Agricultural University, Ghent.

  • Stanghellini, C. (1987). Transpiration of greenhouse crops: an aid to climate management. PhD thesis, Wageningen University.

  • Körner, O. & Challa, H. (2003). Process-based humidity control regime for greenhouse crops. Computers and Electronics in Agriculture, 39, 173–192. doi:10.1016/S0168-1699(03)00079-6

Heating systems & pipe position

  • Kim, H.Y., Yu, O.Y. & Ferrell, J. (2024). Case study: reducing heating energy consumption in a high tunnel greenhouse with renewable energy and microclimate control by bench-top root-zone heating, bench covers, and under-bench insulation. Discover Sustainability, 5, 86. doi:10.1007/s43621-024-00276-5

  • Flores-Velazquez, J. et al. (2019). CFD to analyze energy exchange by convection in a closed greenhouse with a pipe heating system. Revista Mexicana de Ciencias Agricolas, 10(1). SciELO

  • Atilgan, A. et al. (2022). The Economic and Environmental Impact of Greenhouse Heating Pipe Insulation. Sustainability, 14(1), 549. doi:10.3390/su14010549

Photosynthesis & stomatal conductance

  • Farquhar, G.D., von Caemmerer, S. & Berry, J.A. (1980). A biochemical model of photosynthetic CO₂ assimilation in leaves of C3 species. Planta, 149, 78–90. doi:10.1007/BF00386231

  • Medlyn, B.E. et al. (2011). Reconciling the optimal and empirical approaches to modelling stomatal conductance. Global Change Biology, 17, 2134–2144. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2486.2010.02375.x

  • Monteith, J.L. & Unsworth, M.H. (2013). Principles of Environmental Physics (4th ed.). Academic Press.

Crop growth & yield

  • Marcelis, L.F.M. (1994). A simulation model for dry matter partitioning in cucumber. Annals of Botany, 74, 43–52. doi:10.1006/anbo.1994.1093

  • Marcelis, L.F.M. et al. (1998). Modelling biomass production and yield of horticultural crops: a review. Scientia Horticulturae, 74(1–2), 83–111. doi:10.1016/S0304-4238(98)00083-1

  • Seginer, I., Shina, G., Albright, L.D. & Marsh, L.S. (1991). Optimal temperature setpoints for greenhouse lettuce. Journal of Agricultural Engineering Research, 49, 209–226.

  • Heuvelink, E. (1996). Dry matter partitioning in tomato: validation of a dynamic simulation model. Annals of Botany, 77, 71–80.

  • van Henten, E.J. (1994). Greenhouse climate management: an optimal control approach. PhD thesis, Wageningen University.

Plant biophysics (Julia packages)

  • Morales, A. et al. (2025). VirtualPlantLab.jl: a Julia package for functional-structural plant modelling. in silico Plants, 7(1), diaf005. doi:10.1093/insilicoplants/diaf005

  • Vezy, R. et al. (2025). PlantBiophysics.jl: a Julia package for plant biophysical processes. in silico Plants, 7(2), diaf021. doi:10.1093/insilicoplants/diaf021

Photovoltaics & agrivoltaics

  • Faiman, D. (2008). Assessing the outdoor operating temperature of photovoltaic modules. Progress in Photovoltaics, 16(4), 307–315. doi:10.1002/pip.813

  • Boccalatte, A., Fossa, M. & Sacile, R. (2021). Modeling, Design and Construction of a Zero-Energy PV Greenhouse for Applications in Mediterranean Climates. Thermal Science and Engineering Progress, 25, 101046. doi:10.1016/j.tsep.2021.101046

  • Bellone, T. et al. (2024). Agrivoltaic decision support for greenhouse farming. Applied Energy, 369.

Phase change materials in greenhouses

  • Berroug, F. et al. (2011). Thermal performance of a greenhouse with a phase change material north wall. Energy and Buildings, 43, 3027–3035. doi:10.1016/j.enbuild.2011.07.020

  • Benli, H. & Durmuş, A. (2009). Performance analysis of a latent heat storage system with phase change material for new designed solar collectors in greenhouse heating. Applied Energy, 86, 1913–1921. doi:10.1016/j.apenergy.2008.12.029

  • Lazaar, M. et al. (2014). Use of a latent heat storage system for heating a greenhouse in Tunisia. Energy Conversion and Management, 88, 1069–1076. doi:10.1016/j.enconman.2014.09.066

Soil thermal properties

  • de Vries, D.A. (1963). Thermal properties of soils. In: Physics of Plant Environment, North-Holland, Amsterdam.

  • Johansen, O. (1975). Thermal conductivity of soils. PhD thesis, University of Trondheim, Norway. CRREL Draft Translation 637.

  • Kusuda, T. & Achenbach, P.R. (1965). Earth temperature and thermal diffusivity at selected stations in the United States. ASHRAE Transactions, 71(1), 61–75.

  • He, H. et al. (2020). Room for improvement: A review and evaluation of 24 soil thermal conductivity parameterization schemes. Earth-Science Reviews, 211, 103657. doi:10.1016/j.earscirev.2020.103657

Stomatal dynamics under fluctuating light

  • Vialet-Chabrand, S. et al. (2017). Temporal Dynamics of Stomatal Behavior: Modeling and Implications for Photosynthesis and Water Use. Plant Physiology, 174, 603–613. doi:10.1104/pp.17.00125

  • Vialet-Chabrand, S. et al. (2021). The impact of slow stomatal kinetics on photosynthesis and water use efficiency under fluctuating light. Plant Physiology, 186(2), 998–1012. doi:10.1093/plphys/kiab114

  • Elamri, Y. et al. (2018). Water budget and crop modelling for agrivoltaic systems: Application to irrigated lettuces. Agricultural Water Management, 208, 440–453. doi:10.1016/j.agwat.2018.07.001

Chinese solar greenhouses

  • Tong, G., Christopher, D.M. & Li, B. (2009). Numerical modelling of temperature variations in a Chinese solar greenhouse. Computers and Electronics in Agriculture, 68(1), 129–139. doi:10.1016/j.compag.2009.05.004

  • Wei, B. et al. (2016). Thermal performance of single span greenhouses with removable back walls. Biosystems Engineering, 141, 48–57.

FSPM vs simplified crop models (1D vs 3D comparison)

  • Sarlikioti, V., de Visser, P.H.B. & Marcelis, L.F.M. (2011). Exploring the spatial distribution of light interception and photosynthesis of canopies by means of a functional–structural plant model. Annals of Botany, 107(5), 875–883. doi:10.1093/aob/mcr006

  • Sarlikioti, V., de Visser, P.H.B. & Marcelis, L.F.M. (2020). Quantifying the importance of a realistic tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) leaflet shape for 3-D light modelling. Annals of Botany, 126(4), 661–670. doi:10.1093/aob/mcz205

    Key finding: simplified leaf shapes (ellipse, rectangle) deviate only 0.5–1.9% from realistic shapes for light absorption in tomato, and 0.3–0.9% at canopy level.

  • Li, Y. et al. (2021). How does structure matter? Comparison of canopy photosynthesis using one- and three-dimensional light models: a case study using greenhouse cucumber canopies. in silico Plants, 3(2), diab031. doi:10.1093/insilicoplants/diab031

    Key finding: with 3D-calibrated extinction coefficient k, the simple Beer-Lambert model provides equivalent accuracy to 3D ray-tracing for long-term greenhouse simulations. k varies by 0.2 depending on row geometry.

  • Kang, M. et al. (2023). Functional–Structural Plant Model "GreenLab": A State-of-the-Art Review. Plant Phenomics, 0118. doi:10.34133/plantphenomics.0118

  • Chen, B. et al. (2025). Development of a tomato functional–structural plant model for digital twin applications. in silico Plants, 7(2), diaf022. doi:10.1093/insilicoplants/diaf022

Thermal screens

  • Teitel, M. et al. (2009). Energy saving in agricultural buildings through fan ventilation. Energy and Buildings, 41(7), 750–757.

  • Hemming, S. et al. (2017). Evaluation of advanced climate control strategies for greenhouse horticulture. Acta Horticulturae, 1170, 531–538.

Solar radiation decomposition

  • Liu, B.Y.H. & Jordan, R.C. (1960). The interrelationship and characteristic distribution of direct, diffuse and total solar radiation. Solar Energy, 4(3), 1–19. doi:10.1016/0038-092X(60)90062-1

    Reference for the isotropic-sky diffuse-on-tilted-surface model used for cover and wall radiation inputs.

  • Erbs, D.G., Klein, S.A. & Duffie, J.A. (1982). Estimation of the diffuse radiation fraction for hourly, daily and monthly-average global radiation. Solar Energy, 28(4), 293–302. doi:10.1016/0038-092X(82)90302-4

    Clearness-index correlation that splits global horizontal into direct + diffuse when only broadband irradiance is available.

  • Duffie, J.A. & Beckman, W.A. (2013). Solar Engineering of Thermal Processes (4th ed.). Wiley.

Numerical integration & implementation

  • Tsitouras, Ch. (2011). Runge–Kutta pairs of order 5(4) satisfying only the first quadrature condition. Computers & Mathematics with Applications, 62(2), 770–775. doi:10.1016/j.camwa.2011.06.002

    Tsit5 adaptive explicit integrator, used via DifferentialEquations.jl.

  • Bezanson, J., Edelman, A., Karpinski, S. & Shah, V.B. (2017). Julia: A Fresh Approach to Numerical Computing. SIAM Review, 59(1), 65–98. doi:10.1137/141000671. Preprint: arxiv:1411.1607

    Foundational description of Julia’s JIT compilation, type specialisation, and LLVM-based native code generation.

  • U.S. Department of Energy (2024). EnergyPlus Engineering Reference — Climate, Sky and Solar Calculations. Online documentation

    Reference for sub-hourly weather-data interpolation: EPW files are held in memory and linearly interpolated between bracketing hourly samples inside the ODE right-hand side.

Computer-vision dataset (WUR)

  • Hemming, S. et al. (2020). Remote Control of Greenhouse Vegetable Production with Artificial Intelligence — Greenhouse Climate, Irrigation, and Crop Production. Sensors, 20(22), 6430. doi:10.3390/s20226430

    Second Autonomous Greenhouse Challenge (WUR, Bleiswijk). Provides the zenithal RGB + sensor dataset used for LAI ground-truth.

  • Wageningen University & Research. Autonomous Greenhouse / Plant Growth Challenge. autonomousgreenhouses.com

    Official challenge page with datasets (cherry tomato, lettuce, cucumber) and competition rules; the 4th edition (Plant Growth Challenge) is the source of the cam_19 RGB stream.
